raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, Citibank processed a credit card transaction totaling {$10000.00} to XXXX * XXXX XXXX, XXXX DE, charged to my Citibank XXXX ending in XXXX. The transaction was processed through Citibanks system, referencing their merchant XXXX and backend clearing mechanisms. I later submitted a formal dispute after discovering that the merchant, XXXX XXXX XXXX ( aka XXXX Carriers ), failed to deliver contracted services, ceased communication, and became the subject of multiple fraud complaints filed with the FTC, SEC, and FBI XXXX. 

Despite presenting Citibank with evidence of : A non-performing merchant A misrepresented business transaction Public records of other victims and complaints Citibank declined to reverse the charge, citing policy limitations based on the time elapsed, thereby refusing their duty under federal banking regulations, including the Fair Credit Billing Act ( XXXX ) and Bank Secrecy Act ( BSA ), to investigate fraudulent or deceptive merchant behavior. 

This raises serious questions about Citibanks compliance with regulatory obligations, and whether their inaction effectively shields criminal enterprises operating through their payment network. I request that Citibanks failure to act be reviewed alongside the original fraud scheme, as a potential case of institutional negligence or willful complicity.
